what_worked: The hands-on labs were the best part and I could apply them at work the next day.
what_to_improve: Some readings were outdated and did not match the tools we used in class.

what_worked: Instructors gave concrete feedback that actually changed how I approach problems.
what_to_improve: I wanted more practice exercises between the bigger assignments.

what_worked: The cohort format kept me accountable and I made real professional connections.
what_to_improve: The first two weeks moved slowly, then the workload spiked suddenly.

what_worked: Recorded sessions let me catch up when work travel got in the way.
what_to_improve: Clearer rubrics up front would have saved me a lot of guessing.

what_worked: Capstone project was a great portfolio piece I have already shown an employer.
what_to_improve: More examples from non-technical roles would have helped me relate.

what_worked: Office hours were genuinely useful when I got stuck on the tooling.

what_worked: Pacing was steady and the weekly structure made it easy to plan around.
what_to_improve: A bit more time on the capstone would have improved the final result.
